rationale: Adaptive weekly calendar trade. Enter Monday US open (hour 13), early exit Tuesday hour 20 if position is underwater (close < entry price via shift(31)). If profitable, hold until Friday hour 13. Asymmetric payoff: small losses in bear weeks (31h hold), large gains in bull weeks (96h hold). ~13 trades per window with variable hold time to reduce negative Sharpe in bear windows.
new_signals_code:
def compute_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np

    close = df['close']
    hour = df.index.hour
    dow = df.index.dayofweek

    entry_day = int(params['entry_day'])
    entry_hour = int(params['entry_hour'])
    stop_day = int(params['stop_day'])
    stop_hour = int(params['stop_hour'])
    final_day = int(params['final_day'])
    final_hour = int(params['final_hour'])
    stop_lookback = int(params['stop_lookback'])

    is_entry = pd.Series((dow == entry_day) & (hour == entry_hour), index=df.index)

    early_exit_time = pd.Series((dow == stop_day) & (hour == stop_hour), index=df.index)
    position_losing = close < close.shift(stop_lookback)
    early_exit = early_exit_time & position_losing

    final_exit = pd.Series((dow == final_day) & (hour == final_hour), index=df.index)

    entries = is_entry.fillna(False)
    exits = (early_exit | final_exit).fillna(False)

    return entries, exits
